page.title: Sectors Overview
page.subtitle: 12 sectors covering Mexican federal procurement — click any column header to sort
page.rankedBy: Ranked by avg risk score — click to inspect
page.modelNote: Agriculture's elevated risk rate reflects structural market concentration (93% direct awards) and training set composition (Segalmex/DICONSA/LICONSA cases), not necessarily higher corruption prevalence. Health sector carries larger value-weighted risk exposure. Labor (Trabajo) ranks #1 in avg risk due to its small size (48K contracts): GT-labeled vendors from other sectors (DICONSA, Edenred, Toka, AXTEL) hold 31% of its spend, inflating sector averages — not evidence of elevated corruption in labor institutions.

heatmap.title: Sector Risk Heatmap
heatmap.description: Color encodes relative risk ranking per metric — red is highest risk, green is lowest. Click a row to open that sector's full profile.

table.title: Sector Comparison
table.subtitle: Click column headers to sort. Click a sector name to view its full profile.

diagnostico.title: The National Diagnostic
diagnostico.subtitleDynamic: Systemic risk analysis by sector in federal public procurement. {{count}} sectors under surveillance. Results based on {{contracts}} contracts.
diagnostico.subtitleFallback: Systemic risk analysis by sector in federal public procurement. 12 sectors under surveillance. Results based on 3.05 million contracts.
diagnostico.hallazgoText: The Health sector carries the highest absolute risk: ghost-company networks in IMSS, ISSSTE, and COVID-19 emergency purchases represent over $200B MXN in high-risk contracts.
diagnostico.sourceFootnote: Source: COMPRANET 2002–2025, risk model v6.5 · Values normalized min-max per metric
